target_audience.primary_persona: SA/BA and software developers who use AI coding agents and need a structured, repeatable workflow for requirements, planning, implementation, and verification.
secondary_personas: Technical leads standardizing AI-assisted delivery across teams
Solo developers who want stronger context management for agentic coding
AI agent workflow power users working with .agent-based repositories
QA and review specialists who need traceable task artifacts
pain_points: AI coding sessions lose context across phases and tools
Requirements are often discussed verbally or in scattered documents that agents cannot reliably execute
Planning, coding, review, and Git handoff artifacts are inconsistent between projects
Teams using multiple AI IDEs need one portable workflow structure
Large prompts and unbounded context make agent work expensive and error-prone
goals: Turn ideas into structured specs, requirements, plans, implementation logs, and QA reports
Coordinate specialist AI personas for planning, coding, debugging, testing, security, performance, Git, and coaching
Keep task state traceable through JSON and Markdown source-of-truth files
Activate and validate the .agent framework bundle with npm scripts
Monitor task progress through a local PRPs dashboard
target_audience.usage_context: Used inside a software project repository by running slash-command style workflows and helper scripts during AI-assisted development. The framework can be injected, copied, or symlinked into target projects and then activated for the selected AI IDE.

product_vision.one_liner: Nexus-DevFlow is an IDE-agnostic context engineering toolkit that turns AI-assisted software development into a structured, traceable, JSON-driven workflow.
product_vision.problem_statement: AI agents can produce inconsistent results when requirements, project context, planning decisions, implementation status, and verification evidence are scattered across chats and informal documents.
product_vision.value_proposition: The framework gives teams reusable agent personas, slash-command workflows, schemas, scripts, and dashboard tooling so AI IDEs can understand and execute work with clearer context, stronger traceability, and less repeated explanation.
success_metrics: Percentage of tasks that complete the Create -> Plan -> Execute -> Verify workflow with required artifacts
Reduction in rework caused by missing or stale context
Number of projects successfully using the multi-IDE switcher
Validation pass rate for generated JSON artifacts and schemas
Developer satisfaction with planning clarity and agent handoff quality

existing_features: Root npm activation via scripts/activate-agent.mjs for the .agent bundle
Core JSON-driven workflow commands for /30-Task, /31-Plan, /32-Code, and /33-Verify
Specialist agent persona library covering planning, research, coding, frontend, backend, database, testing, debugging, review, security, performance, Git, DevOps, and coaching
Schema and template resources for requirements, implementation plans, context, task logs, metadata, QA reports, and PRP base documents
Root package.json scripts that call the .agent PRP CLI entrypoint
Python helper scripts for task tooling, status, session management, previews, validation, and checklists
Static dashboard assets for task monitoring
Setup documentation for direct install, symlink-based install, and active IDE selection
known_gaps: No top-level docs directory or ROADMAP.md exists
No conventional tests, __tests__, or spec directory was found at the repository root
README and setup content contains mojibake/encoding artifacts in rendered output, especially around Thai and emoji text
Some generated roadmap and discovery files need to stay synchronized between machine-readable JSON and human-readable Markdown
technical_debt: The .agent bundle is now the single active runtime, so any future IDE-specific layout should be generated rather than maintained manually
Project index files are generated under .workspaces and should be regenerated after structural changes
Test scripts exist inside framework folders but the repository lacks a clearly documented root-level validation command
Documentation encoding should be normalized to UTF-8 to preserve Thai instructions and visual symbols
The framework spans Python, JavaScript, PowerShell, Bash, Markdown, and static frontend assets, increasing cross-platform validation complexity

differentiators: Single .agent bundle with npm-based activation and validation
Combines personas, slash-command workflows, schemas, scripts, and dashboard assets in one repository
Centers JSON and Markdown artifacts as the source of truth for AI-agent execution
Designed for SA/BA and DEV collaboration rather than only individual developer prompting
Includes Thai-oriented documentation and personas for local team adoption
competitive_context.market_position: A specialized open/internal productivity framework for teams operationalizing AI-assisted delivery across multiple AI IDEs. It sits between lightweight prompt collections and full software delivery platforms by focusing on repository-local context engineering, traceability, and agent handoffs.

technical: Requires compatible AI IDE behavior for slash commands and agent/rules discovery
Node.js >=18.17 is required for the CLI tooling
Python and PowerShell/Bash scripts need cross-platform testing on Windows, Linux, Mac, and WSL
Symlink setup on Windows can require Administrator privileges
Static dashboard depends on local file layout and generated task data being consistent
resources: Git history shows a small contributor base, suggesting limited maintenance bandwidth
Broad persona and workflow surface area may require careful prioritization to keep docs, schemas, and scripts aligned
No explicit release, support, or governance process was found in the root documentation
dependencies: Git repository access for installation and updates
Target project repositories where the framework is injected or symlinked
Antigravity-compatible .agent workflows and local agent runtimes
Optional browser access for the static dashboard
